 Justification:
 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $5.591M million for Advisory and Assistance Services to promote efficiencies and advance the policies of the Administration in alignment with Executive Order 14222,
 "Implementing the President's Department of Government Efficiency Cost Efficiency Initiative."

 The FY 2026 request was reduced by $6.740 million for civilian personnel to optimize the workforce in compliance with Executive Order 14210, "Implementing the President's Department of Government
 Efficiency Workforce Optimization Initiative."

 SURFACE TRAINING ADVANCED VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENT (STAVE) SEAFRAME SIMULATORS
 FY26 decrease of $4.26M associated with the completion of the FY25 effort to add additional Bridge Part Task Trainers to overseas ports in support of the Navy's overall strategic plan, as well as the realignment
 of funding intended for LCS In-Service Modernization and Combat Systems from individual training procurement account to LCS procurement accounts for LCS Lethality and Survivability systems, Twin Boom
 Extendable Crane (TBEC) reliability improvements, and LCS Combat Systems modernizations.

 CVN 78 Class Training Equipment
 FY26 decrease ($1.594M) supports the planned reduction in Tactical Training Equipment (TTE) requirements of new CVN 78 Class Contractor Furnished Equipment (CFE) systems.

 LCAC Life Cycle Training System
 FY26 decrease ($9.067M) for SSC Life Cycle Training procurement is due to the program accelerating procurement of the east coast full motion trainer into FY25, while providing sufficient funding to conduct
 LCAC legacy trainer modernization in FY26.

 FY26 increase of $21.955M for P5 Surface Training Device Modifications in accordance with the Surface Training Investment Plan (STIP) requirements for the fiscal year. Increase due to significant planned
 investments to include CE 2.1.1 SURFACE MINOR MODS for STAVE Combat Systems Engineering training devices and tech refresh for FY26. This program provides funding to procure, modify, and upgrade
 training devices to keep them compatible with equivalent changes made to Fleet operational equipment. Funds ensure alignment between Surface Learning Centers and Fleet Concentration Area training
 systems and Fleet training requirements as dictated by the Surface Training Investment Plan (STIP).

 Total Ship Training Capability (TSTC)
 FY26 increase ($4.674M) is due to an increase in hardware procurements to support embedded training capabilities of the Surface Combat System Training Environment (SCSTE) and Integrated Combat System
 (ICS).

Line Item MDAP/MAIS Code: 000
 Funds additional hardware procurements and associated installations to implement an onboard training instance of the Legacy Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) Integrated Combat Management System (ICMS) with
 simulations representing actual sensors and weapons to run Joint Advanced Warfare Scenarios (JAWS) training scenarios.
 Funds additional hardware procurements and associated installations to implement embedded training and associated combat system elements for Live, Virtual, and Constructive (LVC) Training Inport and
 Underway for Lethality and Survivability (L&S) Upgrade Littoral Combat System (LCS) Ships.
 Increase in support of procurements for At-Sea LVC capability for DDG 1000 as well as Integration of Naval Strike Missile onto FFG 62 LVC embedded training capability.

 Ready Relevant Learning (RRL)

 Project prioritization reflects strategic emphasis on critical readiness requirements, with lower-priority projects deferred or scaled back accordingly. Funding adjustments for LI 5664 include strategic reductions
 impacting critical training capabilities. FY26 newly accepted reductions include $0.5M in facility modifications, $1.045M due to the loss of one classroom, and $1.9M resulting from the deletion of one PTT lab set.
 These cuts collectively reduce student throughput capacity by approximately 25-30% for NEC V69A training. Additionally, a vertical reduction of $0.63M affects CV-TSC, impacting planned capabilities.

 -PTT Lab Procurement: Reduction from two sets to one, cutting $1.9M, and significantly affecting practical training capabilities.
 -ASTAC Simulator: Reduction to $1.83M delays simulator development, and delivery.
 The FY 2026 request totals $4.764 million to support critical Ready Relevant Learning (RRL) infrastructure and simulation development efforts. Specifically, these funds will enable:
 Virtual Maintenance Trainer (VMT) classroom infrastructure to deliver digitally integrated and instructor-facilitated learning; Part Task Trainer (PTT) labs, which provide hands-on, fleet-relevant skills training
 aligned with updated occupational standards.
 ASTAC Development - SIM, supporting immersive, scenario-based simulation to enhance tactical decision-making and retention.

 These investments are foundational to the continued modernization and scalability of RRL training across the Navy. They ensure the infrastructure and technologies are in place to meet evolving Fleet
 requirements, reduce time-to-train, and increase training effectiveness across enlisted pipelines. The requested funding aligns with strategic priorities to deliver high-impact training at the point of need, sustain
 readiness, and support long-term Navy training transformation.

    FY26 increase of $10.964M is due to an increase in hardware procurements to support embedded training capabilities of the Surface Combat System Training Environment (SCSTE) and Integrated Combat System (ICS). Funds additional
 hardware procurements and associated installations to implement an onboard training instance of the Legacy Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) Integrated Combat Management System (ICMS) with simulations representing actual sensors and
 weapons to run Joint Advanced Warfare Scenarios (JAWS) training scenarios. Funds additional hardware procurements and associated installations to implement embedded training and associated combat system elements for Live, Virtual,
 and Constructive (LVC) Training In-port and Underway for Lethality and Survivability (L&S) Upgrade Littoral Combat System (LCS) Ships.
 (9)
       FY26 decrease of $8.499M for SSC Life Cycle Training procurement is due to the program accelerating procurement of the east coast full motion trainer into FY25.
 (10)
        FY26 decrease of $0.568 provides funds to support the completion of LCAC legacy trainer modernization.
 (11)
      Project prioritization reflects strategic emphasis on critical readiness requirements, with lower-priority projects deferred or scaled back accordingly.                                                        Funding adjustments
 for LI 5664 include strategic reductions impacting critical training capabilities. FY 2026 newly accepted reductions include $0.5M in facility modifications, $1.045M due to the loss of one classroom, and $1.9M resulting from the
 deletion of one PTT lab set. These cuts collectively reduce student throughput capacity by approximately 25-30% for NEC V69A training. Additionally, a vertical reduction of $0.63M affects CV-TSC, impacting planned capabilities.
 -Facility Modifications: Reduced facilities from four classrooms to two, resulting in decreased student capacity by ~25%. -PTT Lab Procurement: Reduction from two sets to one, cutting $1.9M, and significantly affecting practical training
 capabilities. -ASTAC Simulator: Reduction to $0.75M delays simulator development, and delivery, requiring future offset funding. The FY 2026 request totals $4.764 million to support critical Ready Relevant Learning (RRL) infrastructure and
 simulation development efforts. Specifically, these funds will enable: Facilities modifications to prepare and adapt physical spaces for new training systems and learner-centered environments; Virtual Training Management (VTM) classroom
 (12)
    Funding figures and changes in quantities reflect explicit operational and training readiness risks after major decrease on budget liens. RRL maintains alignment with priority readiness goals, through the reduced funding presents
 operational risks and delays in capability delivery.

Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                    -                  -                   -



 Description:
 The Total Ship Training Capability (TSTC)/Advanced Training Domain (ATD)/ Battle Force Tactical Training (BFTT) family of systems provides realistic joint warfare training across the spectrum of armed conflict;
 realistic unit level team training in all warfare areas; a means to link ships together which are in different homeports for coordinated training; external stimulation of shipboard training systems; and simulation of
 non-shipboard forces.
 TSTC/ATD/BFTT uses a distributed architecture, integrating existing training systems, and uses Distributed Interactive Simulation (DIS) and High Level Architecture (HLA) protocols.
 TSTC/ATD/BFTT provides ships' Commanding Officers and Strike Group/Battle Force (SG/BF) Commanders with the ability to conduct coordinated realistic, high stress, combat system level team training as an
 integral part of the Afloat Training Groups (ATGs), the Tactical Training Groups and C2F/C3F Fleet Synthetic Training (FSTs)/Live Virtual Constructive (LVC) exercises.
 The Total Ship Training Capability (TSTC) integrates existing and emergent onboard training and assessment system capabilities to simulate realistic, "train like you fight", combat-like conditions across weapon
 sensor and combat systems.
 Migration to TSTC improvements is required to ensure continued, persistent FST/LVC interoperability to deliver training that is commensurate with tactical capabilities.

 [BFTT/ATD Ship Sets] BFTT AN/USQ-T46 and ATD AN/USQ-T52/T52A are core components of the TSTC.
 Commercial off the Shelf (COTS) Obsolescence mitigates replacement and upgrade of obsolete and out-of-production COTS components in BFTT systems installed throughout the Fleet to include the AN/USQ-
 T46D upgrade.

 BFTT T46 and T52 Ship set configurations vary depending on AEGIS or Ship Self Defense System (SSDS) installation. T46/T52's for SSDS include additional materials and units to be integrated for stimulation
 of the ships sensors.

 TSTC/ATD/BFTT Ship Set funding will procure T46's and T52's and subsequent variants of hardware and software for Build 5.x series in support of AN/SQQ-89 upgrades, AN/SLQ-32(V)6 upgrades, AN/USG-2B
 upgrades, and ATD software builds in support of AEGIS Baseline 9 TI-16 upgrades and SSDS upgrades -- which drives the difference in unit cost.
 Installation funding supports installation of BFTT/ATD Systems.
 Additional costs will be incurred to procure materials needed to ensure cyber security requirements are met and to maintain connectivity with Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE) to meet shipboard
 integrated training demands.

Initial Spares ($ in Millions)                                                                          -                         -                         -                    -                  -                  -
Gross/Weapon System Unit Cost ($ in Dollars)                                                            -                         -                         -                    -                  -                  -



 Description:
 Total Ship Training Capability (TSTC)/ Battle Force Tactical Training (BFTT) family of systems Upgrade Kits procure, install, and remove various quantities of upgrade kit/Engineering Change Proposal (ECP)
 system components, ShipAlts, Field Changes and Software Deliveries on surface ships and related shore sites to support the fleet's needs. Upgrade kits also support Shipboard Connectivity Suite (SCS) Tech
 Refresh and Fleet Synthetic Training (FST) Shipboard Network Connectivity upgrades.

 [BFTT/ATD/TSTC Upgrade Kits] ShipAlts are upgrades driven by Combat Systems configuration as well as training configuration requirements.
 Field Changes include existing BFTT Hardware T46 and Software Baselines 5.0 and 5.1 upgrades, Advanced Training Domain (ATD) T52/T52A hardware upgrades, Battle force Electronic Warfare Trainer
 (BEWT) Baseline II Hardware & Software upgrades, and Training Simulation Stimulation System (TSSS) legacy radar interface upgrades, and Virtual Tactical Bridge Embarked Synthetic Radio (VTBeSR)
 systems.
 Software Deliveries are required to support Combat System, IA/cyber and BFTT/BEWT/BEWT-II/TSTC/TSSS/VTBeSR Hardware requirements.
 Upgrade BFTT/TSTC Shipboard Connectivity Suite (SCS) through a Tech Refresh, to meet Navy Continuous Training Environment (NCTE), interoperability, and cybersecurity requirements.
 Upgrade Shipboard Network Connectivity in support of Fleet Synthetic Training (FST) At Sea capability in support of Strike Group certification training events.
 Upgrades include Information Assurance (IA)/cybersecurity compliance, as well as Obsolescence, Network upgrades and externally driven interface modifications.

 Footnotes:
 (21)
     P40A CEC Training at Sea consolidated to P3A Upgrade Kits line. Unit cost adjusted to reflect lower hardware costs. Quantity increase for additional upgrade kits for LCS in accordance with fleet priorities.
 Install cost increase reflects increasing Planning Yard and Alteration Installation Team (AIT) installation cost requirements, inflation, and upgrade complexity. Unit costs are variable due to the unique software
 and/or hardware required by the specific training capability improvement.
 (22)
     Upgrade kit procurements are on average initiated in first quarter and become available in second quarter of the current fiscal year for installation in third quarter, finishing in second quarter of the next fiscal
 year.
